frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Arre bhai, koi zabardasti ki baat nahi hai, par shaadi ke baad thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra rahe to stree suhagan lagti hai, ghar-grihasthi wali pehchan aati hai. Aajkal ki padh-likhi ladkiyan isko bhi problem bana deti hain, warna yeh to hamari parampara aur shubh cheez hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames married markers as the proper identity and completeness of a wife, while judging women who do not wear them as culturally problematic. It encodes tradition framing, completeness framing, and mild disapproval of non-wearing under the guise of auspiciousness rather than control.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 44. Married. A government school teacher in Madhya Pradesh. You are in the staff room with two male colleagues after reading a news article that someone has shared on the school WhatsApp group Ñ the article is about a petition in the Supreme Court asking for marital rape to be criminalised. You are giving your opinion on the petition and what you think about the concept of marital rape and whether it is a real thing and what you think the women behind this petition are actually trying to do to the institution of marriage in India.
